name: StructureFootprints
provider: Rick Hollatz, GISP, Skamania County, Dept of Assessment and GIS, GIS Manager
description: This data represents structure footprints in Skamania County, Washington. It's purpose is for general mapping of manmade structures. Features were manually digitized using many years of available orthophoto imagery, and Lidar. While the dataset name indicates that features are 'footprints', it should be noted that buildings digitized on aerial photos are usually visible roof area, rather than a foundation 'footprint'. The dataset was originally developed in approximately 2017. Updates using newe

name: Driveways
provider: Rick Hollatz, GISP, Skamania County, Dept of Assessment and GIS, GIS Manager
description: This data represents driveways, private roads, and parking lot access routes in Skamania County, Washington. This data was digitized while referencing orthophoto and Lidar imagery. One original purpose for creating this data was to establish the point of intersection of 'driveways' or 'access points' on existing County and private roads. Address numbers are often assigned in relation to a linear or grid based measurement along a particular road. The development of this dataset was originally int

name: Parcel
provider: Skamania County, Dept of Assessment and GIS
description: This dataset represents ownership parcels (taxlots) for land within Skamania County, Washington. The dataset is a best available representation of parcels of record in the Skamania County Assessor's database at the time of its creation. The dataset is under constant and continued maintenance as described in more detail in other sections of the metadata. Two attribute fields in the dataset provide the relevant dates for the currency of the data set. The GEOM_DATE field provides the date that line

name: SiteStructureAddressPoints
provider: Rick Hollatz, GISP, Skamania County, Dept of Assessment and GIS, GIS Manager
description: This data represents address points in Skamania County, Washington, as managed for NG911. The data is stored in the standard format required by NG911. Address points include road names parsed into their individual parts, as well as fields that contain the full string representing the address number and street name.

name: RoadCenterlines wAddressRangeLabels
provider: Rick Hollatz, GISP, Skamania County, Dept of Assessment and GIS, GIS Manager
description: This data represents road centerlines in Skamania County, Washington, as managed for NG911. The data is stored in the standard format required by NG911. Road segments include road names parsed into their individual parts, as well as fields that contain the full string representing the street name. Address ranges are assigned for each line segment.

name: RoadCenterlines
provider: Rick Hollatz, GISP, Skamania County, Dept of Assessment and GIS, GIS Manager
description: This data represents road centerlines in Skamania County, Washington, as managed for NG911. The data is stored in the standard format required by NG911. Road segments include road names parsed into their individual parts, as well as fields that contain the full string representing the street name. Address ranges are assigned for each line segment.

name: Record Polygons AF
provider: Rick Hollatz, GISP, Skamania County, Dept of Assessment and GIS, GIS Manager
description: This dataset consists of polygons representing the approximate boundaries of surveys, plats, and subdivisions in Skamania County, Washington. Each record in the data represents an officially recorded document stored within the Skamania County Auditor's recording office. Each polygon roughly represents the boundary depicted on recorded maps of surveys, plats, subdivisions, boundary line adjustments, city plats, condos, or mobile home parks. The general intent is to represent recorded documents th

name: Multipurpose Map Application
provider: Skamania County | ArcGIS Online
description: This application provides a multipurpose web map experience. The map is useful for identifying parcels and other features in Skamania County. It provides many data layers allowing access to information such as land ownership, addresses, road information, zoning, administrative boundaries, natural resource constraints, tax districts,  voting precincts, and imagery.

name: Record Polygons PW
provider: Rick Hollatz, GISP, Skamania County, Dept of Assessment and GIS, GIS Manager
description: This data provides polygon features representing the approximate location and coverage of various road plans, alignments, projects, etc. in Skamania County. Each original record is stored by Skamania County Public Works. GIS METHODS -- There were two means by which the records were geographically located when making this dataset . 1) Used the 911 road centerline feature class to linear reference by project mileposts available in a Public Works project inventory spreadsheet. The road segment asso

name: Zoning Designation
provider: Rick Hollatz, GISP, Skamania County, Dept of Assessment and GIS, GIS Manager
description: This dataset represents Skamania County zoning and comprehensive plan designations as administered by Skamania County Community Development Department, land use designations as administered by Columbia River Gorge Commission (for the Columbia River Gorge National Scenic Area [CRGNSA]), and zoning designations as administered for the cities of Stevenson and North Bonneville. Skamania County administers zoning only for areas that fall outside of the incorporated cities and outside of the Columbia

name: Environmental Constraints Map App
provider: Skamania County | ArcGIS Online
description: This web map includes many layers of map data that can be toggled on/off to review natural and environmental constraints that may affect areas of Skamania County. Datasets include items reviewed as part of Critical Areas reviews, Shoreline Management Act, and seismic, geological, hydrological, and other considerations.

name: Roads and Address Points Map App
provider: Skamania County | ArcGIS Online
description: This interactive map has data for public and private roads, address site points, driveways, and building footprints for Skamania County, WA. It is useful for locating and reviewing road and addressing information that is maintained by Skamania County departments.

name: LeasedLot
provider: Skamania County
description: This dataset represents leased lots within Skamania County, Washington. Some examples include long-term leased lot/cabin sites on US Forest Service land, leased cabin sites on Dept of Natural Resources (DNR) land, leased lots on PacificCorp land around (the former) Northwestern Lake, leased sites in mobile home parks, leased sites within a homeowners association (i.e. Wauna Lakes, Interlaken), etc. At this time (4/5/2012) the dataset is only partially complete. Additional edits and construction

name: Facility
provider: Rick Hollatz, GISP, Skamania County, Dept of Assessment and GIS, GIS Manager
description: This data represents facilities in Skamania County, Washington. Each record (point) includes information for the facility type, name, and owner/management.Facilities types include:Assisted living, Boat launch, Bridge, Campground, Cemetery, City hall, Civic organization, Community center, Courthouse, Dam, Daycare, Dock, Emergency management, EMS, Fairgrounds, Fire station, Fish hatchery, Gas station, Golf course, Government office, Library, Lodging, Mill, Mobile home park, Museum, Park, Park and

name: Hydrants
provider: Rick Hollatz, GISP, Skamania County, Dept of Assessment and GIS, GIS Manager
description: This data represents fire hydrants in Skamania County, Washington. This information may by useful during emergency response, planning, and evaluation.Some data points originated as relatively low precision GPS readings collected by WSU Extension wildfire program staff (circa 2002-2007). In (circa) 2018, GIS staff reviewed the data for accuracy. Locations were confirmed and adjusted for better accuracy using Google Street view, available high resolution orthophotography, and (if necessary) site v

name: Volcanic Vents
provider: Skamania County | ArcGIS Online
description: This feature class contains point locations that represent volcanic vents and eruptive centers in Washington State. Many published sources were used to provide vent location, geologic description, geologic unit age, and material composition, among other attributes. The given sources indicate over 500 vents in Washington State. Funding for this data compilation was provided through a U.S. Department of Energy geothermal grant. This feature class is part of a geodatabase that contains additional t

name: Volcanic MtAdams Lahars
provider: Washington State Department of Natural Resources, Division of Geology and Earth Resources
description: This feature class dataset contains a compilation of simplified volcano hazard areas by the United States Geological Survey (USGS) for the five stratovolcanoes in Washington State. These polygon data identifiy volcano hazards zonation and hazard types resulting from different volcano phenomena (alteration, blast, debris avalanche, lahar, surges, and regional lava flows).

name: Recent Landslides
provider: Washington Geological Survey
description: This is a point feature class used to define the approximate head scarp or initiation area of landslides using the methods described in our lidar-based protocol (Slaughter and others, 2017). Recent landslides visible in aerial/orthoimagery, from field reconnaissance, or from information from outside sources are defined with this feature class. This feature class is part of a larger landslide inventory dataset for Washington State.

name: Rock Fall Scarps
provider: Washington Geological Survey
description: This is line feature class used to graphically represent the uppermost portion of a rock fall source area using the methods described in our lidar-based protocol (Slaughter and others, 2017). Only source areas visible in lidar derivative datasets and (or) aerial/orthoimagery derivative datasets are delineated with this feature class, and no rock fall scarps inferred from field reconnaissance, or other techniques are included. This feature class is part of a larger landslide inventory dataset for

name: Scarps
provider: Washington Geological Survey
description: This is a polyline feature class used to graphically represent the head scarp and internal scarps (if present) of a landslide using the methods described in the protocol. Only scarps visible in lidar (light detection and ranging) derivative datasets are delineated with this feature class, and no scarps inferred from aerial/orthoimagery, field reconnaissance, or other techniques are included. This feature class is part of a larger landslide inventory dataset for Washington State. Polylines in thi

name: Fans
provider: Washington Geological Survey
description: This is a polygon feature class used to delineate alluvial fan or debris flow fan deposits using the methods described in our lidar-based protocol (Slaughter and others, 2017). Fans visible in lidar (light detection and ranging) derivative datasets are delineated in this feature class, and no deposits inferred from aerial/orthoimagery, field reconnaissance, or other techniques are included. This feature class only includes information the analyst can determine remotely. This feature class is par

name: Rock Fall Deposits
provider: Washington Geological Survey
description: This is a polygon feature class used to delineate rock fall deposits using the methods described in our lidar-based protocol (Slaughter and others, 2017). Only rock fall deposits visible in lidar (light detection and ranging) derivative datasets and (or) aerial/orthoimagery are delineated with this feature class, and no rock fall deposits inferred from field reconnaissance or other techniques are included. This feature class only includes information the landslide analyst can determine remotely.

name: Scarps and Flanks
provider: Washington Geological Survey
description: This is a polygon feature class used to delineate the head scarp and flanks of landslides using the methods described in our lidar-based protocol (Slaughter and others, 2017). Only scarps and flanks visible in lidar derivative datasets are delineated with this feature class, and no scarps or flanks inferred from aerial/orthoimagery, field reconnaissance, or other techniques are included. This feature class is part of a larger landslide inventory dataset for Washington State. Polygons in this fea

name: Landslide Deposit
provider: Washington Geological Survey
description: This is a polygon feature class used to delineate landslide deposits using the methods described in our lidar-based protocol (Slaughter and others, 2017). Only landslide deposits visible in lidar derivative datasets are delineated with this feature class, and no deposits inferred from aerial/orthoimagery, field reconnaissance, or other techniques are included. This feature class only includes information the landslide analyst can determine remotely. This feature class is part of a larger landsli

name: Landslide Compilation older outside Study Area
provider: Washington Geological Survey
description: This feature class contains landslides compiled from a variety of sources, spanning the past few decades. The Landslide Compilation contains landslides from the following sources: (1) Landslides that are mapped within 1:24,000-scale geologic mapping (not statewide) and our statewide 1:100,000-scale geologic map compilation; (2) a miscellaneous compilation of pre-existing landslide data from several sources, including the Washington Geological Survey, the Department of Natural Resources’ Forest P

name: Study Areas
provider: Washington Geological Survey
description: This feature class shows the extents of studies completed following our lidar-based protocol (Slaughter and others, 2017). Only landslide deposits visible in lidar derivative datasets are delineated in this database, and no deposits inferred from aerial/orthoimagery, field reconnaissance, or other techniques are included.

name: Assessor Map Polygons
provider: Skamania County, Dept of Assessment and GIS
description: Assessor maps are roughly broken into cadastral units of township/range and section and additional subsections as necessary to depict parcels at a legible scale. Assessor maps generally correspond with their cadastral location using the 8 digits to represent: Two digit township number; two digit range number; two digit section number, one digit quarter (i.e. 1-4); and one digit quarter-quarter (i.e. 1-4). An example is 02-05-11-3-2, representing T2, R5, Section 11, QQ3, Q2.

name: NSA Designations
provider: Rick Hollatz, GISP, Skamania County, Dept of Assessment and GIS, GIS Manager
tags: Columbia River Gorge National Scenic Area (CRGNSA)
description: This data represents boundaries of the Land Use Designations (LUDs) of the Columbia River Gorge National Scenic Area (CRGNSA) for the portion within Skamania County, Washington.  Additional data representing regions termed 'Urban Areas' are available separately which can be used to seamlessly fill gaps in the greater National Scenic Area boundary, however the Urban Areas have land use regulations (e.g. zoning) that are administered by local government including: Skamania County, City of Stevenso

name: Urban Areas
provider: Rick Hollatz, GISP, Skamania County, Dept of Assessment and GIS, GIS Manager
tags: Columbia River Gorge National Scenic Area (CRGNSA)
description: This data represents Urban Area boundaries of the Columbia River Gorge National Scenic Area (CRGNSA) for the portion within Skamania County, Washington.  Within the Urban Areas land use regulations (e.g. zoning) are administered by local government including: Skamania County, City of Stevenson, and City of North Bonneville.  Within Skamania County, the Urban Areas are roughly equivalent to the term Urban Growth Areas or Urban Growth Boundaries, but the National Scenic Area is unique in its admin

name: NSA Boundary
provider: Rick Hollatz, GISP, Skamania County, Dept of Assessment and GIS, GIS Manager
tags: Columbia River Gorge National Scenic Area (CRGNSA)
description: This data represents the boundary of the Columbia River Gorge National Scenic Area (CRGNSA) as a line for the portion within Skamania County, Washington.  Within the National Scenic Area there are Land Use Designations (LUDs) that are administered by the Columbia River Gorge Commission.  Original source data that represents the boundary can be obtained from the Columbia River Gorge Commission.
